segments.text: A message about the desert by the sea.  Like storm winds passing through the Negev, something is coming from the desert, from a land of terror—

a horrifying vision that has been explained to me. The betrayer still betrays; the destroyer still destroys. Elamites and Medes, go ahead, attack and lay siege to Babylon,  for I'm putting a stop to all the pain it has caused.
As a result my body is filled with agony. I'm overwhelmed with pain, like the pain of a woman giving birth. I am confused by what I hear; I am distressed by what I see.
My mind falters; I shake in panic. The night I looked forward to has become terrifying.
They set the table, they spread out the rugs, they eat and drink… {
Get up, officers! Prepare your shields for battle!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: This is what the Lord told me: {
Go! Have a lookout keep watch, and make sure he reports what he sees.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: When he sees chariots coming pulled by pairs of horses, riders on donkeys and on camels, he should watch very carefully, paying close attention.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Then the lookout shouted {
Sir, I've stood here on the watchtower day after day; night after night I've remained at my post.
segments.speaker: The lookout

segments.text: Now look! A man in a chariot with a pair of horses is coming.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then he said,
Babylon has fallen, has fallen! All the idols of its gods lie smashed on the ground!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: My poor downtrodden people, so badly mistreated, I have told you what I have heard from the Lord Almighty, the God of Israel.
A message about Edom.  A voice is calling to me from Seir, asking, {
Watchman, what time of night is it? Watchman, what time of night is it?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The watchman replies, {
Morning is coming, but the night is coming again soon. If you want to ask again, then come back and ask.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: A message about Arabia. Caravans from Dedan, spend the night in the bushes.
People of Tema, take water to the thirsty, meet the refugees with food.
They're running away from a fierce battle, from swords, drawn swords, from bows and arrows.
This is what the Lord told me: {
Within one year, just as a contract worker precisely counts years, all the glory of Kedar will be gone.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: There will only be a few left of the archers, the warriors of Kedar.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: The Lord, the God of Israel, has spoken.
